review_title: Simple & effective way to load data into our Azure SQL data warehouse
description: It's a powerful data load tool for organisations that don't want more complicated ETL tools. Customer service is great and I recommend CData Sync.
author: Iain S.
pros: CData sync has proved itself to be a simple & reliable tool for loading data into Azure SQL. It has got a lot of flexibility in terms of how and when the data is loaded. It supports incremental loads. It's powerful and simple at the same time
cons: We wanted to use the Sage50 UK integration but the driver does not expose all the data we needed. I think that is more a limitation of the Sage50 UK API. We now extract our Sage50 UK data with ODBC and then run an incremental load using CData Sync every 15 minutes. It works well.

description: I've only had positive experiences with the product and company - these guys stand behind what they do.
author: Brig L.
pros: This application is incredible. I used to think that syncing a cloud-based application into a local SQL server had to be addressed on a case by case basis. Then I found CData, which allows you to set up connections from multiple popular data sources to various destinations in a flexible and intuitive manner. Selecting tables/fields, scheduling sync jobs, etc. - all of it is very easy! Our needs are limited to syncing a few sources into a local SQL server database, primarily so we can report on it using Tableau. However, the application allows for many more data sources and destinations than we are using. It's clear the product team has made great strides for their app to meet the needs of many. Additionally, the support team is fantastic. They are not only knowledgeable and helpful, but the company has been shown to be very customer-centric by mobilizing development resources as needed to meet our needs through updates to the application on multiple occasions. If you are looking for middleware to sync data between systems - look no further!
cons: The application is locally installed on a machine, as opposed to being cloud-based. Would be nice to access it from anywhere. That said, this hasn't ever really been a hindrance. We have it installed on a server machine which I can remote access from various workstations.

review_title: Cross platform data transfer becomes easy
description: Not happy with the support.Not happy with bugs on SQL, snowflake and Postgres integrations. Same platform data sync works great but struggles with cross platform sync
author: Ravinder B.
pros: Incremental methodology works great if the table has PK and a record modified date
cons: Syncing to snowflake is a challenge for us. Buggy, We were unable to handle the data move in and out to snowflake. Support is not great in fixing the issue. Response time too long.All date columns are messed up, it adds/removes hours arbitralali

review_title: Facilitates Access to CRM in SQL Server
description: We use this every day, and it's very helpful to have data in SQL Server for business critical reporting
author: John M.
pros: Better than SSIS, copy and paste SQL Statements
cons: Some issues encountered with upgrades. Would like a better status dashboard

description: Overall, this tool allowed me to centrally cover all my integration flows in the most efficient way possible
author: Marco R.
pros: CData sync promptly performs the tasks for which it was purchased, user support is of an unrivaled level
cons: The GUI can certainly be improved and there is no "compact" display mode

description: I can rate it 3/5 overall basis. It need more improvement on Large volume Loades.
author: Naveen B.
pros: It's a great tool when implementing new data pull since it has large number of Data Connectors ,Easy to use and Pull data in fraction of seconds.
cons: It's completly slow when pulling Entire data set and Works well only for incremental loads.In few cases it is completly slow for lesser dataset.Logs capturing should give more infomation on Load Progress

author: Ahsan A.
pros: Ease of use to link from SQL server to SAlesForce
cons: It run slow on a large data. may be, add more commands to cover both SF_refresh/SF_Replicate

review_title: The right solution if you want to Synchronize
description: I've been using Cdata Sync for a few months and the experience has always been positive. Surely the investment paid off in no time.
author: Marco R.
pros: The product is very fast, provides a wide range of connectors, both as source and destination, and has top-notch user support.
cons: For some more advanced features, concerning some connectors, such as incremental update, the documentation is not very up to date

description: Good customer support but did not entirely meet expectations
author: Justin D.
pros: Set it up once and requires minimal maintenance. Pricing based on the number of connections makes CData better than competitors.
cons: It may be a QBO API issue, but we are not retrieving all the data we require. Support was helpful but did not resolve the issue

author: Plamen P.
pros: Easy to integrate and setup, yeasy to use
cons: uses a lot of RAM memory resources, the sync is unstable

review_title: Simple to use and very powerful for building your data warehouse
description: Before decided to go with CData Sycn I spent 6 months to trial on many different options and tools. Taking into accounts cost and maintenance, CData Sycn provided the most effective way to bring the Saas database into our on premises Data Warehouse. Setting up trail is very easy as well, you have access to the full feature but limited number of rows to proof the concept.
author: William C.
pros: We use this bring multiple Saas database into our on premises data warehouse (SQL Server). This software is so powerful and easy to use. It does not require deep technical knowledge on how to deploy this. The support team was very helpful during deployment. The documentation of this software is very comprehensive.
cons: The only downside to this is for all the connectors I setup there is no increamental upload which will make the data sync so much more efficient. having to sync the entire table can take a while especially once database grow biggest over the years. I hope CData Sync developer team will bring this feature soon!!

author: Tom B.
pros: easy to use to integrate apis and data services
cons: nothing really, it is very easy to use......

author: Joshua L.
pros: The Ability to integrate all APIs into our core DB system
cons: Nothing so far, all current features meet our requirements

description: Cdata Sync solves our data replication problems and is very easy to use.
author: Patrick I.
pros: The ease with which cdata sync connects to our platforms and recognizes entities is great!
cons: The notifications regarding upgrades are not great.

review_title: Long time vendor for us at Open Text
description: CData from a sourcing perspective was very easy to work with and are a great help in looking to the future and not just the present
author: Spencer K.
pros: Integration was seamless. The business owners at Open Text that use CData are very happy with how the product works
cons: I am not aware of any missing product features

description: We were trying to migrate from CData PowerBI Connector for Sage Intacct 2021 (CTBGA) to a cloud-to-cloud solution, we ended up building a direct connection from Sage using exports.
author: John B.
pros: Easy to set up and run through testing !
cons: Expensive, although you get a lot for the money.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial
